selling to his customers anyway and incidentally there are instances I'm not I'm going to tell you the instance I'm not going to name any of the names some of you would know it so a handful of years ago I had a client who his thing did not work profitably when you sold it direct right advertising we did a bunch of direct mail test stuff that wasn't highly visible but we knew you couldn't make a profit creating a customer from scratch I was pretty sure you could make a profit running it at other people's customers in order to start him going around and making the arrangements to run it at other people's customers we ran big full-page ads in the trade journals where he advertises knowing we were gonna lose our ass the only reason we had the ad there was so the five people he wanted to do joint ventures with sought that we just wanted to send a message to the five people we're doing joint ventures with the ads were as expected dismal failures the joint ventures worked out pretty well I don't know that he'd have got the joint ventures if they hadn't seen that ad in there for three months so this is a while ago now but so I had this client I think I mentioned him yesterday it's death control expert and so to supermarket and convenience store industries so we wanted to leverage the state associations so every state there's a grocers association and there's a convenience store owners association in some big markets there's even metro ones LA has its own association for example it's big enough New York has its own association so really there's about I don't know 62 63 convenience store owner associations and maybe 60 supermarket or associations and so we wanted to leverage these associations into holding full-day seminars I didn't want Jack speaking on their program at their regular meeting I wanted a full day with everybody in a room so we get some of that stuff and I didn't want the limitations of you can't sell from the stage out so we wanted a freestanding event promoted by really meaning through the association with the associations impronter on it endorsement of it this was not going to be easy to get because Jack was really really controversial and he also had already pissed off a fair number of the association executives so we picked three places and did the seminar by filling the seats ourselves now I knew doing it we're gonna lose money I mean I knew it didn't make any difference so I don't remember the numbers now but I would say we probably I don't know probably spent 40,000 bucks to bring in 10 and pretty much knowing that was going to happen to start with but I needed the associations to see this happening in the marketplace and I picked places where even as a dismal failure you'd wind up with a fairly big number of people in a room based on the size of the market and of course I overspent to put them there then we went to all the state associations and said this seminar is coming to Iowa it just was done in Pittsburgh coming to Iowa we'd like to do it with the Iowa State Grocers Association and here's how cool that arrangement is if for some reason you guys don't want to do it we're coming to Iowa in August and we're gonna do it so that's pushy it then you got to fix the relationship after you get the deal but it got the deals and so we did almost every association in both of those industries over a two-year period and again you had to fix these relationships afterwards now the second element of this is everything was turned key and this is back to whatever point I made number two for two reasons one the association busy they got other stuff to do they don't need a seminar to market secondly they're incapable of doing it and you'd have four people in a room
